reviews.review_title: The spider man game we waited for
reviews.review_description:
Immaculate swinging mechanics, grappling hooking around feels great. Combat is okayish, with no augments implemented (like bullettime, homing shot, etc) you are basically locked out of the coolest move - shooting from mid-air. Unless you are committing to death... which is not a great game design wise.
Unfortunately the first boss is one of the hardest as well. The game pushes you to fight from covers but the boss can turn so fast, making you unable to hit the weakpoint on the back.
Oh and the mob spawning moves of bosses are a literal waste of time. You have to kill like 20 each round, and they spawns every 10s...

4.5/5

The combat while engaging is definitely the weakest part of the game. Not much enemy variety and gets repetitive having to move, aim, and shoot the vulnerable spots over and over again. The story is nothing to write home about but it’s charming and simple along with the cartoony graphics. The biggest selling factor is the parkour along with the amazing swinging mechanics that feel great and responsive.

I absolutely adored the first Windlands and it’s “take it at your own pace and relax or speedrun” style. Windlands 2 decides to add combat to a parkour game? And then take a way a bunch of the parkour. To top it off they add npcs with quests and unskippable dialog that’s just god awful. I played through it all the way once and I think thats about all I’ll play of it. Ive played the original Windlands so many times, this way ultimately just kind of a crappy Windlands experience.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the honest feedback — and for the love you showed the original Windlands.

Windlands 2 was a deliberate shift toward a more structured, co-op-focused experience, with combat and NPCs added to support that direction. We know that meant moving away from the pure, open-ended flow and minimalist feel that many players loved in the first game, and for some people that just won’t land the same way.

Totally fair take, and we appreciate you giving it a full playthrough and sharing your perspective. Feedback like this helps us reflect on what resonated most about the original experience and we do love it.

reviews.review_description: People Bailey play
I absolutely love this game , but on multiplayer it crashes,and it has no save ,so I have to start all over again 🤨

developer_response.body:
Hi GamecatValerie

Sorry about that frustration.

Just to clarify: in multiplayer, if you join another player, you’re playing on the host’s save. Your own progress only advances when you are the host.

If you were hosting, your game should save normally, so a crash there isn’t expected. If that happens again, please email us at support@psytecgames.com
 with your platform details and we’ll look into it.

Thanks for the kind words about the game 💙

reviews.review_description:
I absolutely love this game. Had it on my  HTC but lost it when I switched to Quest.  Recently bought Quest 3 and excited to find Windlands2 available.  Still love it but it has bugs now.  

Please fix the door panels!!!. Half of them will not respond to a direct hit with an arrow!!!. Frustrating.

reviews.review_title: One of the best VR Games
reviews.review_description: If you have the VR legs for it that is. Looks and plays fantastic on quest 3! Devs are always updating the game  were quick to respond to a bug I was encountering.

The new gameplay in Windlands 2 & the vast new areas in which to explore are beyond amazing. Having more things to do in the main quest instead of just searching for tablets is an enjoyable experience (though that doesn't mean Windlands 1 is anything less than awesome as it is). The only drawback for me for Windlands 2 (which I would hope is an easy update or fix) would be that there is not enough upward  momentum when you are scaling the walls. In Windlands 1 when you clawhook up the structures within the game you can send yourself soaring above them after your last clawhook. In Windlands 2 you don't really get that momemtum which is what made Windlands 1 so exhilarating. On the last clawhook in the upwards direction you kind of get pulled back and don't experience that final soar above the structures. Thanks for listening & I can't wait to get the new game you are developing. Kind regards!!

Of course it's a great idea for a game but instead of devs telling players how to deal with motion sickness in vr, they should be looking at how similar games have achieved a wonderful sick free experience.

It is difficult to look for objects when you have to focus on the next tree you can hook, it's also not helpful when you lose momentum, because all you can do is fall, can't jump up like in windlands 1. Trying to swing a little with thr thumb stick whilst stuck hanging there is useless you can't hardly move. 
Trying to swing up onto a platform I cannot figure out at all, it's impossible to get collectables that are on a platform above you, can't climb up can jump and defo cannot swing up in the little semi circle shape gaps on the platforms. 

It's all a bit repetitive as well, I'm tired of going back to speak to the same characters travelling back and forth and having to figure out how to get there, I'm lost most of the time tbh.

Controls are simple and easy, graphics not great but colourful.

Having to play in 10min intervals because of the motion sickness but having to stop all the time in game to see when I'm meant to be going so the sickness is actually less than I experienced in windlands 1.

Not attempted multiplayer as most people say you can never match make as there aren't any online players.

I adored this game on my quest 2 however this crashes when booting up the game on my quest 3, I've also factory reset my device already, but still it doesn't load and 8 have to perform a hard reset on my quest 😭

developer_response.body:
We’ve seen a small number of Quest 3–specific startup issues and are actively investigating. Could you please email us at support@psytecgames.com
 with:

Confirmation you’re on Quest 3

Your OS version (if you know it)

Whether the crash happens immediately on launch or after the splash screen

That’ll help us pinpoint it quickly. Really appreciate you flagging this, especially after enjoying it on Quest 2 💙

reviews.review_description: AMAZING with friendsssss!!!! :DDDD

I just hopped back on after a long while, this is honestly (in my opinion) among the best Vr games ever the graphics hold up well, great fps, amazing boss fights, great story, and so much more, if your somehow reading this and don’t have the game, GET IT! But one eensy weensie problem is that no one plays the game anymore which is kinda sad but yk whatever. K byeee

Great game, just like the first part. Windlands 2 contains many innovations such as larger worlds, opponents, lots of battles, tasks, etc. The epic battle with the giant sand worm was an amazing experience. If you haven't bought it yet, I highly recommend starting with the first part of the game. Both parts provide an amazing experience, but if you start with the second part, you may find the first part a bit boring, since there are no fights there. If there will be Windlands 3 (I hope), I will definitely buy it.

Bought this in a bundle because the description was interesting, bought it lacks a good coop community. Only found one active game online and kept failing to connect, so tried to return it, but Meta claims that it is not eligible for refund, even though I didn't play it more than Hour, which was mostly tutorial, 1st task, and several failed attempts at joining the one active coop game. Selling my Meta stock tomorrow.

Played for about 30 minutes and felt extremely nauseous the entire time. The nausea did go down a bit but would come back randomly and with a vengeance. I tried sitting first because I felt like I would fall over standing. Once I got comfy ish sitting back I tried sitting forward so I could look around easier and felt nauseous all over again. Still feel sick 10minutes later.

developer_response.body:
Sorry you are effected by motion in VR, Windlands 2 is marked as comfort:intense on the store, good advice is 
 to start off slowly with lower Intensity,5 minute sessions and Ginger Beer can also help gain your VR legs before tackling the more intense games. https://www.meta.com/en-gb/help/quest/articles/accounts/purchasing-apps/oculus-store-comfort-rating/

reviews.review_description: How do you save your progress?

developer_response.body: Hi cuphe.2023, Progress is automatically saved at every checkpoint and mission progress point, Enjoy!
